Document:
# Terror Spiders
Terror Spiders are a genetically engineered bioweapon created from giant space spiders spliced with Xenomorph DNA. They rely on venom, ambushes, webs, and vents to overwhelm the station. Their strength varies wildly depending on their caste, and a coordinated hive can quickly overrun whole departments.
They are weak to ranged weapons and fire, but extremely dangerous in melee and in the dark. Every spider benefits from their psionic link, letting them sense death, communicate, and coordinate instantly.
## So I'm a spider, so what?
Terror Spiders are fast melee creatures that excel at ambushing isolated crew members. Your biggest strength revolves around your webs, which paralyze anyone who steps on them.
Nearly all spiders are capable of vent crawling, allowing rapid traversal of the station. They also gain extra permanent regeneration by wrapping humanoid creatures in webs.
You also have a caste. Different castes play extremely differently, so your first priority is to understand which spider you are.
# Terror Spider Castes
Below are all castes, progressing from the lowest tier one, up to tier four.
## Red Terror
Red Terrors are slow but extremely durable frontline spiders. Their melee damage increases as their own health decreases, making them extremely dangerous when wounded. - Slowest spider. - Deals more damage the lower its health is.
## Gray Terror
Gray Terrors are ambushers that become nearly invisible while on webs. - Semi-invisible while on webs. - Deals double damage to webbed or snared targets. - Low durability. - Weak in a straight-up fight.
## Green Terror
Green Terrors are breeders that create egg clutches. - Lays egg clusters onto cocoons that hatch into spiderlings. - Weak in direct combat. - Only capable of creating more tier one spiders.
## Black Terror
Black Terrors are poison specialists. - Extremely lethal venom. - Victims must immediately receive toxin treatment or die. - Not particularly good in a direct confrontation.
## White Terror
White Terrors are infectors that implant eggs directly into victims. - Bites implant eggs that hatch after a delay. - Up to five spiderlings can burst from the body. - Excellent for spreading chaos and growing the hive rapidly.
## Purple Terror
Purple Terrors are heavy defenders. They are much like red terror spiders, except stronger. - Must stay close to the Queen, if they stray too far for too long, their psionic link will break and they will explode into gibs. - Very tanky and strong. - Breaks walls and doors. - Best used as a linebreaker or nest guard. - Cannot ventcrawl.
## Brown Terror
Brown Terrors specialize in vent and obstruction clearing. - Can force open welded vents. - Weakest combat spider. - Pure utility. - Cannot ventcrawl.
## Prince of Terror
A Tier three miniboss spider, designed for slaughter. - High damage, high health. - Can break most barriers. - Uses thick webs to pin victims. - The most dangerous spider to fight. - Cannot ventcrawl.
## Princess of Terror
A royal-layering spider that accelerates hive growth. - Can lay eggs anywhere just like the Queen. - Very fragile without protection. - High priority target for the crew, as the only alternative to reliably laying high-tier eggs besides the queen.
## Mother of Terror
A nurturing brood spider. - Generates royal jelly that can be eaten to heal rapidly. - Boosts hive sustainability. - Weak in direct fights, almost entirely centered around utility. - Cannot ventcrawl.
## Queen of Terror
The apex of the hive. - Produces eggs that have higher chances of spawning high tier spiders the more creatures the hive has wrapped. - Extremely strong in a fight inside her territory. - Killing the Queen disrupts the hive’s coordination and growth.
# Fighting Terror Spiders
The spiders may seem overwhelming, but they have exploitable weaknesses.
- Use ranged weapons. All spiders with the sole exception of the queen are slow and capable of fighting only within close-range. - Weld vents and scrubbers. - Avoid fighting in the dark or in webs. - Kill breeding spiders (Green, White, Princess, Queen) first, as they can make the hive quickly grow out of control. - Fire and spacing are extremely effective, though keep in mind spiders can always just relocate when cornered.
# Playing Terror Spiders Well
- Stick with the hive. Lone spiders die fast. - Break lights. Darkness is your ally. - Wrap downed crew for regeneration for the whole hive. The more dead crew you manage to wrap, the stronger the hive will become. - Ambush from vents. - Protect egg-layers at all costs. - Use webs to slow down their advance and trap groups of crew. - Coordinate kills; swarm isolated targets.
